violations.text: Establishment's Rhode Island Certified Food Safety Manager license is expired. FMC to re-instate. Food safety certificate is current. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2026

Raw animal foods such as raw steak was observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as lettuce in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets for the walk-in cooler and walk-in freezer were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2025
The guard for walk-in cooler evaporator has an accumulation of dust. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2026
The floor tiles in the public restroom area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2026

Several ceiling tiles in the restroom and dining area were observed in poor repair (due to water damage). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2026
The following were in poor repair: -gaskets for walk-in cooler -lid for flour bin. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2025

Chicken, Pepperoni and Bacon were observed held at 47 and 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Food was observed stored above fill line in prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview of staff, foods were recently placed into unit. Food was divided into more containers and placed under refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was observed at 111 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Food was placed on to sheet pans to continue cooling process. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2024
